observations.recommendation:
1.9 We recommended and Management agreed to direct the Municipal
Accountant and other concerned offices to:
a. Prioritize the compliance with the preliminary activities required under
COA Circular 2020-006 for the one-time cleansing of PPE accounts to
establish the cost of the actual and existing PPE items of the municipality;
and
b. Initiate the disposal of the unserviceable properties pursuant to Section
379 of R.A. No. 7160 and in accordance with the procedures cited in COA
Circular 89-296.

2.10 We recommended that Management require the Municipal Accountant and
Municipal Treasurer to:
a. Conduct a thorough review and reconciliation of the CIB – LCCA
account taking into consideration the deficiencies noted in their records
and substantiate the previous years’ lump sum amount reflected in the
BRS and subsequently prepare the necessary adjusting entry to establish
the correct balance thereof.
b. Regularly conduct reconciliation of accounts in order to effect the timely
recording of the necessary corrections in the books and consistently
harmonize the balances of both records.

3.9 We recommended and Management agreed to:
a. Require the Municipal Treasurer and the Inventory Committee to fast
track the conduct the physical inventory of all the drugs and medicines
and other supplies and materials in accordance with existing rules and
regulations to facilitate the recording of the correct inventory account
balances in the financial statements and submit the required report
thereon to the Auditor concerned not later than July 31 and January 31
of each year for the first and second semesters, respectively; and
b. Direct the Municipal Treasurer / Property Officer to maintain the
required stock cards and ensure that the reported balances are reconciled
with the supplies ledger card maintained by the Accounting Office.

4.12 We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the:
a. Municipal Treasurer to prepare the duly certified list showing the names
of the taxpayers and the amount due and collectible for the year based
on the RPTAR or Taxpayer’s Index Card maintained at the Municipal
Treasurer’s Office and furnish the Municipal Accountant;
b. Municipal Accountant to record the receivables based on the certified list
furnished by the Municipal Treasurer’s Office including the 25 per cent
share for the Barangay and 35 percent Province share for RPT-Basic as
well as the 50 per cent Province share for SET in order to recognize in
the books an accurate and reliable balances pursuant to 20 of the
MNGAS for LGUs, Volume 1.
c. Municipal Accountant to prepare the appropriate journal entries
following the pro-forma entry for the setting up of the receivable,
receipts of payments and the distribution in order to come up with the
correct balance of the affected accounts as at December 31, 2024.

5.9 We recommended and Management agreed to instruct the incumbent
Municipal Accountant in coordination with the Municipal Treasurer to:
a. Conduct reconciliation of the account based on all available supporting
documents to ensure that the reported unexpended balances from CY
2015 up to present are correct and the actual transfers of cash from GF to
TF from CYs 2015 to 2018 is properly supported; and
b. Prepare the necessary adjusting entry for any differences noted in order
to reflect the correct balance of TL-DRRMF account as of year-end.

6.9 We recommended and Management agreed to instruct the Municipal
Accountant to coordinate with the Municipal Treasurer and expedite the
actual transfer of the unexpended balance of LDRRMF to the TF books in
the amount of ₱2,474,672.15 and record the same using the following
proposed adjusting journal entry:
In General Fund Books
Prior Period Adjustment ₱2,474,672.15
Cash in Bank- LCCA ₱2,474,672.15
To record the transfer of unspent LDRRM funds
In Trust Fund Books
Cash in Local Treasury ₱2,474,672.15
Trust Liability –DRRM ₱2,474,672.15
To record the receipt of unspent LDRRM funds from General Fund

7.7 We recommended and Management agreed to direct the Municipal
Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer to ensure that the transfer of the
unexpended balance of the LDRRMF to the unappropriated surplus of the
General Fund is in accordance with COA Circular No. 2012-002.

8.9 We recommended and Management agreed to instruct the Municipal
Accountant to conduct review and analysis of the accounts with abnormal
balances and prepare the necessary adjustments in order to present the
correct balances of the accounts in the financial statements.

9.6 We recommended and the Municipal Accountant agreed to ensure that the
transactions of the municipality were recorded using the appropriate
accounts pursuant to the descriptions provided for in COA Circular Nos.
2015-009 and IPSAS 1.

10.6 We recommended and Management agreed to instruct the Municipal
Accountant and the concerned municipal official to exert effort to retrieve
and locate the supporting documents to facilitate the recording of the
liquidation of the confidential fund; and send demand letters to concerned
accountable officers with unliquidated cash advances.

11.6 We recommended and the Municipal Mayor agreed, in coordination with
the LDRRM Officer, to submit an LDRRMFIP that includes the projects
and activities to be charged against the unexpended LDRRMF of the
previous years, in accordance with the provisions of COA Circular No.
2012-002.

12.6 We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal
Treasurer to release the RPT share of the Barangays, on a quarterly basis,
pursuant to Section 271 (d) of RA No. 7160 in order for the latter to utilize
the funds for its priority projects.
12.7 The Municipal Treasurer informed that the above RPT shares were already
released to the respective barangays in February 2025.
12.8 The Audit Team will monitor on the consistent compliance of the Management
on the release of RPT share of barangay within the required period pursuant to
Section 271 (d) of RA No. 7160.

13.8 We recommended and Management agreed, through the Municipal
Accountant to make an inquiry with GSIS Cebu Branch relative to the
incurrence arrearages despite the over-remittance of ₱47,336.90 and
reconcile the ₱91,894.95 discrepancy in the Due to GSIS account and make
the necessary adjustments based on the outcome of the reconciliation.

14.7 We recommended and Management agreed to direct the Municipal
Accountant to:
a. Conduct review and analysis of the Due to BIR account to ensure the
correctness of the recorded unremitted amount; and
b. Properly monitor the account and ensure that the taxes were remitted
within the prescribed period.

15.5 We recommended and Management agreed, through the Municipal
Engineer, to ensure that the Certificate of Final Acceptance be issued only
after the lapsed of the defects liability period which is one-year from the
date of project completion in order to protect the interest of the
municipality.

16.10 We recommended that the Municipal Mayor through the Municipal
Accountant and concerned officials submit a written justification on the
noted deficiencies and likewise submit a copy of the required authority from
the DILG for evaluation by the Audit Team. Henceforth, we recommend
that Management strictly adhere to the rules and regulations in the conduct
of study tours or “lakbay aral” prescribed under DILG-DBM JMC No. 2.
16.11 The Municipal Local Government Officer pointed out that the submitted CAP-
DEV 2022-2024 to the Audit Team for audit was erroneously given by the LGU
personnel, thus as proof, she handed the revised CAP-DEV 2023-2025
attributable to the new administration in which that Lakbay-Aral was included in
the said CAP-DEV. However, she has yet to follow up the authority from the
DILG allowing the personnel to conduct the said Benchmarking (Lakbay-Aral).

17.8 We recommended and the Municipal Mayor and other concerned officials
agreed to submit the:
a. Sangguniang Bayan Resolution approving the release of the Financial
Assistance since the appropriation thereof was not included in the Annual
Budget in CY 2024;
b. MOA between the Municipality, represented by the Municipal Mayor,
and the recipient League that outlines the manner of utilization,
liquidation, and reporting requirements;
c. Approved Project Expenditures/Estimated Expenses indicating the
project objective and expected output; and
d. Copy of Official Receipt issued by the LMP to the Municipality of
Malabuyoc acknowledging the receipt of funds transferred.

18.11 We recommended and the BAC through the BAC Secretariat agreed to
observe the deadline set by the GPPB on the submission of approved APP,
PMR and APCPI to the GPPB pursuant to GPPB Circular No. 02-2020 to
increase and enhance the transparency on Municipality’s procurement
transactions.

observations.reason: The Management has submitted their justification and was forwarded to RTAG, however, the result of the re-evaluation of the RTAG is yet to be received by the Audit Team.

observations.reason: Implemented as part of GAD mainstreaming efforts.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Treasurer and
the Municipal Accountant-Designate to reconcile the balances, identify the items
that need adjustments and record them appropriately in the affected books.

Further, investigate the root cause of this inefficiency to properly address the issue
and prevent recurrence.

We recommended and the Municipal Accountant-Designate agreed to prepare and
submit the supporting schedules of the controlling accounts in the general ledger
and to maintain updated subsidiary ledgers and records of accounts duly supported
with complete documents to substantiate balances in the financial statements.

Further, review the personnel complement and ensure that offices are manned by
sufficient and competent personnel to meet deadlines and submit complete reports
to the Commission.

We recommended and the Management agreed to direct the Inventory Committee
to hasten the physical count of PPE as of December 31, 2021, render the report
thereon and provide a copy of the report to the Audit Team pursuant to Section 124,
Volume 1 of the Manual on the NGAS for LGUs. Henceforth, consider the timing in
conducting physical count to be able to meet the deadline and ensure that the
RCPPE is properly and completely accomplished.

Also, we recommended and the Management agreed to instruct the Municipal
Treasurer and/or Property and Supplier Officer to maintain property cards, and
Property Ledger Cards in the Accounting Office to facilitate monitoring and
reconciliation.

We recommended and the Municipal Mayor agreed to direct the Accounting Unit to
trace the date of acquisition of the said assets and compute the depreciation. Prior
years’ depreciation should be taken up as Prior Years Adjustment to be closed to
Government Equity and the current year should be recorded as expense.

We recommended and the Municipal Accountant-Designate agreed to carefully
analyze the accounts with negative balances and identify the causes thereon and
effect the necessary adjustments to reflect the correct balances of the above-
specified accounts in order to fairly present the financial statements.

We reiterated our recommendation and the Municipal Accountant-Designate
agreed to prepare a reconciliation report of reciprocal accounts and to take up the
necessary adjustments in the books of accounts to ensure that all reconciling items
are resolved and/or adjusted on a timely basis. Also, perform constant review of the
transactions to deter errors and omissions in order to present the financial
statements in accordance with IPSAS 1.

We recommended that Management ensure that priority projects are timely carried
out and the fund is optimally utilized with the view of effecting efficiency, economy
and effectiveness in the disposition of government resources in compliance with the
provisions set forth in DILG-DBM JMC No. 2017-1 and 2020-1.

We recommended and Management agreed to submit the Contract with duties and
responsibilities acknowledged by the coordinators, duly verified accomplishment
reports and a valid explanation on the non-submission of the necessary documents.
Henceforth, direct the Municipal Accountant-Designate to ensure that all types of
disbursements shall be supported with sufficient relevant documents to establish the
validity and propriety of the claim.

We recommended that Management:
• Require the three individuals to return the corresponding amount of
educational assistance whose conditions and status of eligibility is contrary
to the provisions of DSWD Memorandum Circular No. 11 Series of 2019;
and
• Henceforth, adhere to the guidelines set by DSWD on granting AICS, and
limit only the assistance to vulnerable and marginalized individuals and
families.

We recommended that the Management direct and ensure that the Municipal
Accountant-Designate submit the subject Bank Reconciliation Statements.
Furthermore, the Management makes representation with LBP/DBP to receive the
bank statements in a timely manner or consider the other options to be able to
submit the BRS on time to facilitate the audit.

We recommended that Management initiate the process of recruitment to fill in the
mandatory and other vacant positions with the appropriate qualifications so that
efficiency and effectiveness in the discharge of the functions can be attained. Further
Management is enjoined to observe the rules and regulations on designation as
mandated by the Civil Service Commission.

We recommended that Management require the HRMO-Designate to initiate the
process of recruitment to fill in the position of the Municipal Accountant. The Head
of the Accounting Unit requires the appropriate qualifications so that efficiency and
effectiveness in the discharge of the functions of the unit can be attained. Further,
Management is enjoined to observe the rules and regulations on designation as
mandated by the Civil Service Commission.

We recommended and the Management agreed to direct the Local Disaster Risk
Reduction and Management Officer to prepare the Report on Utilization of DRRM
Fund in accordance with Annex B of COA Circular No. 2012-002.

We also recommended and the Municipal Accountant-Designate agreed to transfer
the unexpended/unobligated balance of the QRF and DRRMF-MOOE to Trust
Liabilities - DRRMF Account in accordance with Item 5.1.10 of COA Circular No.
2012-002.

We recommended and the Management agreed to direct the Municipal Accountant-
Designate or other responsible official of the municipality to prepare a separate
Registry for Appropriations, Allotments and Obligations for LDRRMF to improve
the monitoring on the availability of the disaster fund and to ensure the reliability
and quality of financial information relative to the said fund.

Management direct the Accountant to carefully analyze the accounts with negative balances and effect the necessary adjustments to reflect the accurate balances of the specified accounts in the financial statements with the financial reporting framework adopted.

observations.recommendation:
Municipal Accounting Office be directed to determine if all these checks were indeed released to the proper payees.  Confirmation and/or notices should be sent to the respective payees/creditors so as to verify the receipt of said checks and to inquire as to why these have not been presented to the bank for payment.  

Also, have stale checks still in the hands of the Treasurer, cancelled and replaced according to the guidelines set forth in Section 59 of NGAS Manual, Volume I and coordinate with the Accounting Office for proper recording in the books. observations.recommendation: We recommended and Management agreed to require the Municipal Treasurer and the Municipal Accountant to reconcile the balances, identify the items that need adjustments and record them appropriately in the affected books.

We recommended and Management agreed that should the same condition occur in the future, they should effect the required adjusting entry at the end of the year to restore cash equivalent of unreleased checks to fairly present the Cash in Bank-Local Currency, Current Account in the financial statements.

We recommended and Management agreed to direct the Offices of the Municipal Accountant and Treasurer to work closely to reconcile the differences noted in their respective reports and records and submit reconciliation reports to the concerned auditor.  We also recommended that the Treasurer be directed to maintain Property Cards for each class of property, plant and equipment to be regularly reconciled with PPE Ledger Cards maintained by the Accounting and with Inventory Report duly signed by the Inventory Committee.
Further, concerned officials were enjoined to commence the process of disposal of unserviceable properties in accordance with the guidelines set forth in COA Circular No. 2020-006 and make sure that the properties presented under PPE are only those assets used in the operation of the LGU to present more accurately the balances of PPE in the financial statements.

We recommended and the Municipal Accountant agreed to compute and record the depreciation in the books of accounts in order to present fairly the value of Infrastructure Assets and other affected accounts in the financial statements.

We recommended and the Municipal Accountant agreed to submit the subject bank reconciliation statements. We also recommended that Management make a representation with LBP/DBP to receive the bank statements in a timely manner or consider the other options to submit the BRS on time to facilitate the audit.

We recommended that the Management ensure optimum utilization of the 20% of the Annual Internal Revenue Allotment and fast-track development projects to provide immediate benefits to the constituents and help achieve desirable socio-economic development and environmental outcomes as required under the JMC.

We recommended that the Bids and Awards Committee (BAC) submit an explanation/justification on the non-posting of the updated APP, NOA, and other information relative to the Emergency Procurement under the Bayanihan Act in the GPPB Online Portal as mandated in GPPB Circular No. 1-2020.
Henceforth, we recommended that the BAC should always be updated with the issuances and advisories of the GPPB by visiting their website regularly to avoid non-compliance in the future.

We recommended that Management submit an explanation on what have transpired during the process and make representation with the DSWD as to the proper action on this matter. We also recommended that Management send demand letters to the ineligible beneficiaries for the refund of the amount received totaling ₱732,000.00 and ensure that these disbursed funds are collected and returned to DSWD.

We recommended and the BAC agreed to submit justification for undertaking the Emergency Procurement under the Bayanihan Act without complying with the requirements of GPPB Circular No. 01-2020 and to be always updated with the issuances of the GPPB as the governing board for all the procurement activities of the government.

We recommended that Management establish clear cut guidelines on the selection and grant of AIFCS which may be aligned with the DSWD Memorandum Circular No. 02, series of 2014.  We also recommended that Management submit a written explanation on the deficiencies noted for the Audit Team’s evaluation and validation of the claims.

We recommended and Management agreed to submit a valid explanation on the employment of Shopping modality instead of Negotiated Procurement (Small Value Procurement) and supporting documents such as PhilGEPS Registration, Statement of the Prospective bidder that it is not blacklisted, and Sworn affidavit, Mayor’s/Business Permit, PhilGEPS Registration Number, Income/Business Tax Return, and Omnibus Sworn Statement required in Appendix A and COA Circular 2012-001 to establish validity of the claims.

We recommended and Management agreed through the BAC to submit a written explanation on the failure to adhere with the procedures for procurement of consultancy contract involving an individual consultant.

We recommended and Management agreed to submit the Memorandum of Agreement between the Municipality and the concerned Peoples/Non-Government Organizations for evaluation purposes as well as its Monitoring Report on the projects undertaken by the latter. We also recommended that Management submit the Certificate of Registration, Articles of Incorporation, Work and Financial Plan, Financial Status, Fund Utilization Report and other lacking documents as mentioned in the preceding paragraphs.
Further, we also recommended that the Municipal Accountant make an adjusting journal entry to properly record the grant.
Henceforth, we recommended that Management comply with the provisions of COA Circular No. 2007-001 prior to the release of funds.

We recommended and Management agreed to desist from adopting the reimbursement basis as mode of procurement of goods or services in contravention of Republic Act No. 9184 and strictly adhere to the requirement that all payments must be made by check which are directly paid to the suppliers, contractors, dealers and creditors.  Likewise, submit written explanation for the non-compliance of the R.A. 9184 on the above-mentioned procurements.
It is further recommended that Management withhold the prescribed creditable tax in all of its procurement of goods or services in accordance with BIR Revenue Regulations No. 11-2018.

We recommended and the Municipal Accountant agreed to require the aforementioned supporting documents from all departments that facilitated the receipt and distribution of in-kind donations, prepare the required summary/list and submit to the audit team with the supporting documents. The one-time separate reports for cash and in-kind donations shall also be prepared and submitted to the NDRRMC through the OCD.  It is also recommended that recording of donations be done, if warranted.
We recommended and the Municipal Accountant agreed to create a special account for BGCM in accordance with LBC No. 125.
We recommended and Management agreed to comply with Section 8 of RA 11313 and Section 9 of its IRR to ensure enforcement of the provisions of the Safe Spaces Act.

Management direct the Municipal Accountant–Designate to submit an explanation on why borrowing costs incurred in 2015 to 2018 for the loan availed were only recorded in 2019.  We also recommended that the Municipal Accountant prepare an adjusting entry to present Interest Expenses in the 2019 financial statements reflecting only the current borrowing costs while the borrowing costs pertaining to the period 2015 to 2018 be recorded as prior period adjustment.

observations.recommendation:
We recommend that the concerned Accountant/Bookkeeper be directed to
submit the subject Bank Reconciliation Statements together with the written
explanation on the delayed submission thereof.

We recommend that the Municipal Accounting Office be directed to
determine if all these checks were indeed released to the proper payees.
Confirmation and/or notices should be sent to the respective payees/creditors so as
to verify the receipt of said checks and to inquire as to why these have not been
presented to the bank for payment.

Also, have stale checks still in the hands of the Treasurer, cancelled and
replaced according to the guidelines set forth in Section 59 of NGAS Manual,
Volume I and coordinate with the Accounting Office for proper recording in the
books. Stale checks that are already released to the payees shall only be cancelled
and replaced upon presentation of the stale checks. Henceforth, the Office of the
Municipal Treasurer should send a written notice to the payee at least one month
before a check becomes stale, reminding him of the existence of the outstanding
check.

We recommend that management augment the personnel at the Accounting
unit to assist the Accountant in the maintenance of accurate subsidiary ledgers as
basis for the preparation of subsidiary schedules to support the general ledger
balances.

It is also recommended that management review its personnel complement
and ensure that offices are manned by sufficient and competent personnel to meet
deadlines and submit complete and timely reports.

We reiterate our recommendation that the Local Chief Executive:
a. Direct the inventory committee to conduct physical count of PPE and
prepare the Report on the Physical Count of Property, Plant and
Equipment for submission to the concerned auditor;
b. Require the designated Property Custodian and the designated Municipal
Accountant to maintain complete, accurate and updated property cards
and ledger cards, respectively, for reconciliation and control purposes;
and
c. Instruct to label all properties/equipment, showing complete
information/description for easy identification and monitoring.

We recommend that management cause the submission of the proof of
posting of invitation in the PhilGEPS website and other required documents for the
aforecited transactions. Otherwise, submit a valid explanation on why such
documents could not be produced.

We recommend that management submit the basis/guidelines for the grant of
monthly allowance to more than five hundred (500) community disaster volunteers
including the contracts of service and accomplishments reports prepared by the
volunteers themselves to establish validity of the claims. It is also recommended
that concerned officials submit proof that the CDVs were accredited by the OCD.

We recommend that management release the shares of real property taxes to
barangays within five days after the end of every quarter pursuant to Section 271 of
the Local Government Code.

We reiterated our recommendation that Management adhere and comply
with the pertinent provisions of COA Circular Nos. 2009-001 dated February 12,
2009 and 96-010 dated August 15, 1996 on the prompt submission of contracts and
supporting documents, purchase orders, work orders and notices of deliveries to
ensure timely evaluation and verification thereof.

We recommend that management eventually hire qualified personnel once
financial resources allow to occupy the position of three (3) DRRM Staff to
complement the Local DRRM Officer and properly discharge the functions and
responsibilities of the LDRRM office in accordance with Joint Circular No. 2014-1
dated April 4, 2014.

We recommend that management allocate at least 1% of its IRA for the
strengthening and implementation of programs, projects and activities advocating
child rights separate from its allocation for other social services and use the fund
pursuant to DILG Memorandum Circular No. 2012-120 dated July 4, 2012.
